# External coding-agent pilot — 2026-07-21

Five pinned SWE-bench Verified tasks were attempted from exact base commits with
the same issue-only prompt and graded by the official Docker harness. Prices are
API-equivalent estimates from models.dev token rates; Claude Code used a Max
subscription, so its reported USD is comparative rather than marginal billing.

| Arm | Resolved | Mean wall time | Estimated cost |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| AgentJ / Luna, low effort | 4/5 | 51s | $0.482 |
| AgentJ / Luna, medium effort | 5/5 | 75s | $0.599 |
| Codex / Sol | 5/5 | 76s | $1.367 |
| Claude Code / Opus 4.7 | 5/5 | 76s | $2.213 |
| Claude Code / Fable 5 | 3/3 | 115s | $3.238 |
| OpenCode / Luna | 5/5 | 139s | $1.248 |

Fable quota was exhausted after three tasks; the other two cells are unavailable,
not failures. All 23 primary-run patches were non-empty and all CLI processes
completed without timeout. The initial models.dev estimate was $3.01 for 25
runs; observed agent context overhead produced $8.55 across the 23 completed
primary runs. Extrapolating the two missing Fable runs gives about $10.7.

## AgentJ diagnosis

Low-effort AgentJ missed `sympy__sympy-13647`. It replaced lazy construction
with an eager entry list and added a test that reproduced the broken output.
The hidden `ShapingOnlyMatrix` regression still failed. Every reference agent
found the actual one-line offset bug (`j - other.cols`).

A controlled retry changed only Luna reasoning effort:

- high: resolved, 187s, $0.354;
- medium: resolved, 62s, $0.125.

Running medium across all five tasks resolved 5/5 for $0.599. It tied the best
quality result, cost less than half of Codex, and cost about the same per
resolved task as low effort. The product default therefore remains Luna but
moves from low to medium reasoning effort.

The benchmark also exposed a measurement gap: one-shot AgentJ sessions did not
persist usage records. They now use the same append-only usage ledger as
interactive sessions, preserving the existing session-log abstraction.
